subject: Sociology
title: Slavery as Stratification
description: A fast breakdown of Slavery as Stratification for builders.

hook.text: Twelve million Africans were shipped across the Atlantic in chains. Slavery was stratification at its most extreme.

buildup.text: Slavery is the most rigid form of stratification: one group legally owns another as property.

discovery.text: It existed in ancient Rome, West Africa, and the Americas. The Atlantic system was uniquely racial and industrial.

twist.text: Abolition ended legal slavery, but its economic legacy persists. Wealth gaps created by slavery still shape outcomes.

climax.text: Slavery didn't vanish. It transformed into sharecropping, convict leasing, and systemic racial inequality.

punchline.text: Slavery ended on paper. Its economic shadow never left.

quiz.question: Why does the legacy of slavery still affect stratification?
options: Wealth gaps created by slavery persist across generations
Slavery had no long-term economic effects
